2606.06779.title: Mind the Gap: Bridging Behavioral Silos with LLMs in Multi-Vertical Recommendations
2606.06779.paper_summary: In multi-vertical e-commerce platforms like DoorDash, relatively newer product verticals such as grocery and retail present a significant opportunity for personalization innovation. A key challenge lies in solving the "cold start" problem for users. This paper introduces a novel framework for enhancing recommendation quality by transferring knowledge from data-rich verticals (e.g., restaurants at DoorDash) to data-sparse ones. We leverage Large Language Models (LLMs) to perform generative inference, synthesizing sparse, high-dimensional features that encapsulate latent user affinities. Specifically, we employ a hierarchical Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline to derive multi-level taxonomic features from user restaurant order histories and search queries. These generated features, encoding both long-term cross-vertical preferences and short-term intent, are integrated into a production Multi-Task Learning (MTL) ranking model. We demonstrate through extensive offline and online evaluation that this approach significantly improves personalization and engagement in emerging business verticals, effectively bridging the behavioral data gap.
2606.06779.paper_summary_zh: 在像 DoorDash 這樣的多垂直電子商務平台中，相對較新的產品垂直領域，例如雜貨和零售，為個性化創新提供了重要的機會。 一個主要挑戰在於解決用戶的「冷啟動」問題。 本文介紹了一種新穎的框架，通過將知識從數據豐富的垂直領域（例如 DoorDash 的餐廳）轉移到數據稀疏的領域來提高推薦質量。 我們利用大型語言模型（LLMs）進行生成推理，合成稀疏的高維特徵，這些特徵封裝了潛在的用戶偏好。 具體而言，我們採用層次化的檢索增強生成（RAG）管道，從用戶的餐廳訂單歷史和搜索查詢中推導出多級分類特徵。 這些生成的特徵編碼了長期的跨垂直偏好和短期的意圖，並被整合到一個生產中的多任務學習（MTL）排名模型中。 我們通過廣泛的離線和在線評估證明，這種方法顯著改善了新興商業垂直領域的個性化和參與度，有效地彌補了行為數據的差距。
2606.06779.author: Nimesh Sinha et.al.
2606.06779.authors: Nimesh Sinha, Raghav Saboo, Martin Wang, Sudeep Das
